sections.heading: Architecture Overview
sections.content:
The doc manager is implemented in `docs.js` (2505 lines) — the largest frontend module. It provides a full DocJSON viewer and inline editor within the viz dashboard.

**Layout:** Two-panel: folder tree sidebar on the left, document content area on the right.

**Backend endpoints:**
- `GET /api/docs` — list all DocJSON files (with optional tag filter)
- `GET /api/docs/{id}/raw` — raw JSON for editing
- `GET /api/docs/{id}/render` — rendered markdown + section metadata
- `PUT /api/docs/{id}` — save full JSON back to disk
- `POST /api/docs` — create new doc file
- `POST /api/docs/{id}/move` — rename/move file
- `POST /api/docs/mkdir` — create subfolder
- `POST /api/docs/rescan` — re-scan docs directory
- `POST /api/docs/import` — import Markdown as DocJSON
- `GET /api/docs/subdirs` — folder tree data

**Key features:**
1. Folder tree navigation with expand/collapse persistence (sessionStorage)
2. Rendered view with markdown rendering (including Mermaid diagrams)
3. Click-to-edit sections: textarea for content, inline heading editor
4. Link picker: search cortex nodes and attach/detach links per section
5. Tag management: add/remove chips at doc and section level
6. Section management: add, remove, reorder
7. Mermaid diagram editor with Monaco + live preview
8. Raw JSON source toggle
9. Wide mode toggle (persisted in localStorage)

sections.heading: Key Interactions
sections.content:
### Open Document
```
User clicks doc in folder tree:
  1. GET /api/docs/{id}/render → rendered markdown + section data
  2. GET /api/docs/{id}/raw → raw JSON (for editing)
  3. Render content area: rendered markdown view
  4. Build TOC from sections (including nested sub-sections, indented)
  5. Mark _dirty = false
```

### Edit Section
```
User clicks edit icon on a section (any depth):
  1. Replace rendered HTML with <textarea> containing raw content
  2. Auto-resize textarea
  3. On blur or save: update _rawDoc section content (walk tree by depth path)
  4. Mark _dirty = true
  5. Re-render that section only (optimistic)
```

### Save Document
```
User clicks Save button (appears when _dirty=true):
  PUT /api/docs/{id}  body: _rawDoc (full JSON tree — no reconstruction needed)
  Mark _dirty = false
  Re-fetch rendered view
```

### Link Picker
```
User clicks link icon on a section (any depth):
  Open search overlay
  User types partial node ID or name
  GET /api/search?q=...  →  results
  User clicks a result → add to section.links[]
  Mark _dirty = true
```

### Add Sub-section
```
User clicks "Add Sub-section" on a parent section:
  1. Validate depth < 2 (max depth)
  2. Create new section object: {id: auto-slug, heading: "New Section", content: ""}
  3. Append to parent's sections[] array in _rawDoc
  4. Mark _dirty = true
  5. Re-render: new section appears indented under parent
  6. Auto-open heading edit on new section
```

### Collapse/Expand Section
```
User clicks ▼/▶ toggle on a parent section:
  1. Toggle collapsed state in Set (sessionStorage-persisted)
  2. Hide/show child section DOM elements
  3. Update TOC: hide/show indented child entries
  4. Collapsed header shows "N sub-sections" summary
```

sections.heading: Nested Sections
sections.content:
Sections can recursively contain sub-sections via an optional `sections` array, up to 3 levels deep (depth 0, 1, 2).

**Rendering:**
- Parent sections render with a collapse/expand toggle (▼/▶) in the section header
- Children are visually indented under their parent with a left border line
- Each depth level gets progressively smaller headings: `##` → `###` → `####`
- Collapsed parents show a summary: "N sub-sections" (with stale count if applicable)

**TOC integration:**
- Sub-sections appear indented in the right-side TOC, mirroring the document tree
- The existing `IntersectionObserver` for scroll tracking extends to nested sections
- TOC items at each depth get left-padding proportional to their level

**Section management:**
- Each parent section shows an "Add Sub-section" button (hidden at depth 2 — max depth)
- "Add Section" at the bottom of the document adds top-level sections (unchanged)
- Drag reorder is scoped to siblings within the same parent — you cannot drag a child out to become a top-level section
- Remove section on a parent removes all its children (with confirmation)

**Data flow:**
- `_rawDoc` already contains the tree structure (recursive `sections` arrays in JSON) — no reconstruction needed on save
- `_renderedData.sections` from the server is flat with `parent_id` and `depth` — the frontend builds the tree for rendering
- PUT `/api/docs/{id}` sends the full tree JSON (unchanged from current full-doc save)

**Editing nested sections:**
- Click-to-edit (Tiptap or textarea) works the same at any depth
- Link picker, tag management, and Mermaid diagram editor are depth-agnostic
- Heading inline edit works at any depth

sections.heading: Implementation Trade-offs
sections.content:
### 2500-line monolith
Docs.js handles rendering, editing, link management, tag management, folder operations, Mermaid editing, and raw view. This is large but avoids the complexity of splitting state across modules when all doc operations share the same `_rawDoc` object.

### Textarea editing vs WYSIWYG
Section content is edited in a plain textarea rather than a WYSIWYG editor. This keeps the implementation simple and preserves markdown formatting exactly. The Mermaid diagram editor is the exception: it uses Monaco for syntax highlighting with live preview.

### Full JSON save (PUT)
Every save sends the entire JSON document rather than section-level patches. This avoids conflict resolution at the cost of slightly larger payloads. For typical DocJSON files (<50KB), this is negligible.

sections.heading: Technical Verification
sections.content:
- Folder tree renders correct hierarchy and persists expand/collapse state
- Click-to-edit opens textarea with correct content
- Save writes changes to disk and re-renders correctly
- Link picker search returns relevant nodes and adds to section.links
- Tag add/remove updates chips and marks dirty
- Section add/remove/reorder updates TOC
- Mermaid diagrams render in preview and update live in Monaco
- Wide mode toggle persists across page loads
- Raw JSON toggle shows formatted source
- Nested sections render with indentation and left border line at each depth
- Parent sections show collapse/expand toggle (▼/▶)
- Collapsed parent shows "N sub-sections" summary with stale count
- Sub-sections appear indented in the right-side TOC
- "Add Sub-section" button appears on sections at depth < 2
- "Add Sub-section" button is hidden at depth 2 (max)
- Removing a parent section removes all children (with confirmation)
- Drag reorder works within same parent level only
- `_rawDoc` tree structure round-trips correctly through save/reload
